title: Leptorkis cuspidata
intro: Leptorkis cuspidata, also known as Indian stinging bush or poison bush, is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and small thorns that contain a toxic sap. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Leptorkis cuspidata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Leptorkis cuspidata is often used to prepare herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as ointments and creams, although its use is generally cautioned due to its toxic sap.

uses: Leptorkis cuspidata, also known as the Nepal cinnamon or Nepali cinnamon, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
